title: fire_multiple_ignitions
description: Multiple simultaneous wildfire ignitions are discovered across a wide area, raising suspicion of deliberate arson.

timeline.title: First Ignition Reported
timeline.description: Initial fire call received. Crews dispatched to grassland ignition near the highway.

timeline.title: Second Ignition Confirmed
timeline.description: A second simultaneous fire is reported 4 miles away. Arson suspicion arises.

timeline.title: Third Ignition Identified
timeline.description: Third fire reported. Pattern suggests deliberate ignition. Law enforcement notified.

timeline.title: Resource Split Decision
timeline.description: Available resources must be split across multiple fires. Triage priorities required.

timeline.title: Investigation Ongoing
timeline.description: Investigators on scene of earliest ignition. Preserve evidence while conducting suppression.

timeline.title: All Fires Contained
timeline.description: All three fires contained with mutual aid. Arson investigation ongoing.

node-1.title: Multiple Fires Reported
node-1.situation: Within 30 minutes, three separate wildfire ignitions have been reported across a 15-mile area. The ignitions appear simultaneous and their pattern does not match any known natural cause. Arson is suspected.
node-1.question: How do you respond to multiple simultaneous ignitions?

options.label: A
options.text: Declare a complex incident, notify law enforcement of suspected arson, request mutual aid, and assign crews to each ignition based on threat level.

options.label: B
options.text: Focus all resources on the largest fire and monitor the others until reinforcements arrive.

options.label: C
options.text: Treat each fire independently without coordinating a unified command.

node-2.title: Resource Triage Across Fires
node-2.situation: Available resources can only adequately cover two of the three fires. Fire 1 threatens a farm, Fire 2 is in open land, and Fire 3 is closest to a residential area.
node-2.question: How do you triage fire assignments?

options.label: A
options.text: Prioritize Fire 3 (residential threat) and Fire 1 (structure risk), and use air tankers to slow Fire 2 until ground resources arrive.

options.label: B
options.text: Split all resources equally across all three fires regardless of threat level.

node-3.title: Evidence Preservation
node-3.situation: Fire investigators arrive at the earliest ignition point. Fire suppression activities may destroy arson evidence at the point of origin. Investigators request a buffer zone.
node-3.question: How do you handle evidence preservation during active suppression?

options.label: A
options.text: Establish a small preservation zone around the suspected ignition point and route suppression activity around it while keeping safety as the top priority.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow full suppression operations at the origin point — fire safety overrides evidence collection.

node-4.title: Suspect Reported Near Fire Area
node-4.situation: Law enforcement reports a suspicious vehicle observed near two of the three ignition points. The suspect may still be in the area and could ignite additional fires.
node-4.question: How do you coordinate with law enforcement?

options.label: A
options.text: Share vehicle and location details with all field crews, coordinate with law enforcement for containment, and increase aerial observation to detect new ignitions.

options.label: B
options.text: Leave suspect identification entirely to law enforcement and focus only on fire suppression.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: All three fires have been contained with mutual aid. The suspect was apprehended by law enforcement. Evidence was preserved at the origin point for the arson investigation.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
